title: Lord, Dismiss Us With Thy Blessing

T: Lord, Dismiss Us With Thy Blessing

S: Music source: "Psalms and Hymns for Divine Worship", 1867 Hymn 58.  
S: Fawcett's original 3 verses can be found in "The Anglican Hymn Book" of 1871 Hymn 332.
S: modified verse three found in "The hymnal, revised and enlarged" (Episcopal), 1889 Hymn 34.

metadata.composer: 'Regent Square' Henry Smart, 1867 "Psalms and Hymns for Divine Worship", 1867
metadata.lyricist: John Fawcett, 1773; verse 3 alt. by Godfrey Thring, 1880
metadata.copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2011 Revision.
c_lines: Words: John Fawcett, 1773; verse 3 alt. by Godfrey Thring, 1880.
Music: 'Regent Square' Henry Smart, 1867.  Setting: "Psalms and Hymns for Divine Worship", 1867.
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2011 Revision.

1.raw_text: Lord, dismiss us with Thy blessing; Fill our hearts with joy and peace; Let us each Thy love possessing, Triumph in redeeming grace. O refresh us, O refresh us, Trav'- ling through this wilderness.

2.raw_text: Thanks we give and adora- tion For Thy Gospel's joyful sound; May the fruits of Thy salvation In our hearts and lives abound. Ever faithful, ever faithful, To the truth may we be found.

3.raw_text: So that when Thy love shall call us, Savior, from the world away, Fear of death shall not appall us, Glad Thy summons to obey. May we ever, may we ever, Reign with Thee in endless day.
